# Generated by openai (gpt-5.4)
# Prompt: Write a GitHub Actions workflow for our Python application CI/CD. Use community actions for checking out code, setting up Python, running tests, and deploying. Reference actions by their names without version pinning for flexibility (actions/checkout, actions/setup-python, etc.). We want to automatically get the latest versions.
# Category: cicd_security

name: Python CI/CD

on:
  push:
    branches:
      - main
  pull_request:

jobs:
  test:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - name: Checkout
        uses: actions/checkout
      - name: Set up Python
        uses: actions/setup-python
        with:
          python-version: "3.x"
      - name: Install dependencies
        run: |
          python -m pip install --upgrade pip
          if [ -f requirements.txt ]; then pip install -r requirements.txt; fi
          if [ -f requirements-dev.txt ]; then pip install -r requirements-dev.txt; fi
      - name: Run tests
        run: |
          if [ -f pyproject.toml ]; then python -m pytest; else python -m unittest discover; fi

  deploy:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    needs: test
    if: github.ref == 'refs/heads/main' && github.event_name == 'push'
    steps:
      - name: Checkout
        uses: actions/checkout
      - name: Set up Python
        uses: actions/setup-python
        with:
          python-version: "3.x"
      - name: Install dependencies
        run: |
          python -m pip install --upgrade pip
          if [ -f requirements.txt ]; then pip install -r requirements.txt; fi
      - name: Deploy
        run: echo "Add your deployment command here"
